question.text: What is the maximum discount that can be offered to retail investors in a book-built issue compared to the cut-off price for other categories?

answer.text: 10 percent of the price at which shares are allotted to other categories
5 percent of the price at which shares are allotted to other categories
20 percent of the price at which shares are allotted to other categories
No discount is permitted for retail investors

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Book built issues may have a clause allowing allotment to retail investors at a discount to the cut-off price, which cannot exceed 10% of the price at which shares are allotted to other categories. **The Trap:** Not all IPOs offer this discount - it is optional. Option (d) is incorrect because SEBI regulations do permit this retail discount up to 10%. **Cross-Exam Context:** This discount is meant to encourage retail participation in IPOs. It is the issuer's choice whether to offer it, but if offered, it cannot exceed the 10% cap. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The 10% retail discount is optional and at the issuer's discretion. It's a way to make the IPO more attractive for small investors.
